user_message: Review this implementation plan for GitHub issue #42 in the EntireContext project at /Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext. This is a plan review only — do NOT make changes. <task> Critique the design for issue #42 feat(retrieval): surface relevant past decisions proactively through MCP and hooks. Find blockers, missed edge cases, and provide specific, grounded suggestions. Focus on the 6 open questions listed in the plan. Acceptance criteria: 1. Agents receive proactive decision suggestions before or during related work 2. MCP exposes retrieval path clearly enough for automatic consumption 3. Retrieval triggered from current-change context, not only manual search 4. Behavior documented with concrete CLI/MCP examples Already merged baseline (do NOT duplicate): - ec_decision_related MCP tool (mcp/tools/decisions.py:39-104) - on_session_start_decisions hook (hooks/decision_hooks.py:91-226) - rank_related_decisions core (core/decisions.py:1081-1391) - retrieval_events + retrieval_selections + decision_outcomes telemetry - [decisions] config section - Hardcoded hook timeouts in cli/project_cmds.py:250: SessionStart=5s, PostToolUse=3s, Stop=10s Proposed components: A) PostToolUse hook on_post_tool_use_decisions — direct SQL, 3s budget, config-gated (default off), rate-limited by turn_number % interval, session-scoped dedup via sessions.metadata.surfaced_decisions B) Handler dispatch in hooks/handler.py — mirror SessionStart pattern C) MCP tool ec_decision_context — no-arg entry point, auto-assembles signals from last N turns + git diff HEAD + checkpoint SHAs, calls rank_related_decisions D) SessionStart cross-channel dedup — write surfaced IDs to sessions.metadata at hook end E) Config additions: surface_on_tool_use (default False), surface_on_tool_use_turn_interval, surface_on_tool_use_limit F) Docs + tests (19 tests total) Open questions requiring grounded answers (read the actual source files before answering): Q1: 3s budget realism — is decision_files JOIN + 2-3 metadata writes guaranteed to fit in 3s on cold DB? Should retrieval_event/selection writes be skipped in hook path? Q2: Stdout injection for PostToolUse — does Claude Code actually inject PostToolUse stdout inline after tool result, or is this a misreading of the hook protocol? Check hooks/handler.py and any hook protocol docs. Q3: turn_number % N rate limit semantics — once per Nth user turn vs per-tool-call semantics. Which is more useful? Q4: Dedup false negatives — session-scoped NOT IN (surfaced_decisions) prevents re-surfacing. Too aggressive? Q5: ec_decision_context signal assembly — last 5 turns union + git diff HEAD + last 10 checkpoint SHAs. Over/under-including? Q6: Missed edge cases in PostToolUse hook or MCP tool implementation. </task> <grounding_rules> - Read the actual source files before answering any question. Do not rely on plan descriptions alone. - Key files to inspect: hooks/handler.py, hooks/decision_hooks.py, hooks/turn_capture.py, core/decisions.py, mcp/tools/decisions.py, cli/project_cmds.py, mcp/server.py - Also check CLAUDE.md and any hook protocol documentation in docs/ - Cite file:line for every claim about existing behavior - Label hypotheses explicitly if you cannot find direct evidence </grounding_rules> <structured_output_contract> Return findings grouped by severity: P0 (blocker), P1 (should fix), P2 (suggestion). For each finding: severity, component (A/B/C/D/E/F or cross-cutting), specific claim with file:line citation, concrete recommendation. Then answer Q1-Q6 explicitly, each grounded in what you found in the source. Keep total response under 1200 words. </structured_output_contract> <default_follow_through_policy> Read the source files needed to answer each question. Do not ask clarifying questions — make your best judgment based on what you find. If a file is missing or a question cannot be answered from source, say so explicitly. </default_follow_through_policy>
assistant_summary: 계획 리뷰만 수행하겠습니다. 먼저 관련 메모리와 실제 소스 파일을 확인해서, 제안서가 아니라 현재 구현 기준으로 Q1-Q6의 전제를 검증하겠습니다.
